 {"id":"bd-37i","title":"[PLAN 10.21] Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)","description":"Section: 10.21 — Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)

Strategic Context:
BPET longitudinal phenotype intelligence track for pre-compromise trajectory detection and integration with DGIS/ATC/migration/economic policy.

Execution Requirements:
- Preserve all scoped capabilities from the canonical plan.
- Keep contracts self-contained so future contributors can execute without reopening the master plan.
- Require explicit testing strategy (unit + integration/e2e) and structured logging/telemetry for every child bead.
- Require artifact-backed validation for performance, security, and correctness claims.

Dependency Semantics:
- This epic is blocked by its child implementation beads.
- Cross-epic dependencies encode strategic sequencing and canonical ownership boundaries.

## Success Criteria
- All child beads for this section are completed with acceptance artifacts attached and no unresolved blockers.
- Section-level verification gate for comprehensive unit tests, integration/e2e workflows, and detailed structured logging evidence is green.
- Scope-to-plan traceability is explicit, with no feature loss versus the canonical plan section.

## Optimization Notes
- User-Outcome Lens: "[PLAN 10.21] Behavioral Phenotype Evolution Tracker Execution Track (9O)" must improve operator confidence, safety posture, and deterministic recovery behavior under both normal and adversarial conditions.
- Cross-Section Coordination: child beads must encode integration assumptions explicitly to avoid local optimizations that degrade system-wide correctness.
- Verification Non-Negotiable: completion requires reproducible unit/integration/E2E evidence and structured logs suitable for independent replay.
- Scope Protection: any simplification must preserve canonical-plan feature intent and be justified with explicit tradeoff documentation.","notes":"Acceptance Criteria alias (plan-space normalization, 2026-02-20):
- The `Success Criteria` section in this bead is the canonical acceptance contract for closure.
- Closure additionally requires linked unit/integration/E2E verification evidence and detailed structured logging artifacts from all child implementation  {"id":"bd-383z","title":"[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow.","description":"Master Plan Source: PLAN_TO_CREATE_FRANKEN_NODE.md
Section: 10.17 — Radical Expansion Execution Track (9K)

Why This Exists:
Radical expansion track for proof-carrying speculation, Bayesian adversary control, time-travel replay, ZK attestations, and claim compiler systems.

Task Objective:
Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Real incident traces can be replayed and compared against synthesized mitigations with expected-loss deltas; promoted mitigations require signed rollout and rollback contracts.

Expected Artifacts:
- `docs/specs/counterfactual_incident_lab.md`, `tests/lab/counterfactual_mitigation_eval.rs`, `src/ops/mitigation_synthesis.rs`, `artifacts/10.17/counterfactual_eval_report.json`.

- Machine-readable verification artifact at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-383z/verification_evidence.json` for CI/release gating.
- Human-readable verification summary at `artifacts/section_10_17/bd-383z/verification_summary.md` linking implementation intent to measured outcomes.
Testing & Logging Requirements:
- Unit tests for core logic, invariants, and error handling.
- Integration/E2E tests for end-to-end control-flow behavior.
- Detailed structured logs/telemetry with stable codes and trace correlation IDs.
- Deterministic replay fixture or reproducible artifact for failure analysis.

Task-Specific Clarification:
- For "[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow.", preserve full capability scope from the plan; no feature compression or silent omission is allowed.
- Verification for "[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow." must include capability-specific thresholds and machine-readable pass/fail evidence artifacts.
- Unit tests, integration/E2E scripts, and structured logs for "[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow." must support deterministic replay and root-cause triage without hidden context.

Why This Improves User Outcomes:
- "[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow." is required to reduce operator error, increase deterministic safety guarantees, and improve time-to-diagnosis for failures in real-world workflows.
- Explicit success/failure evidence for "[10.17] Build counterfactual incident lab and mitigation synthesis workflow." prevents false confidence and improves trust in migration/compatibility/security outcomes.
- Detailed unit + integration/E2E + structured logging requirements ensure reproducible quality and faster incident remediation for users.","acceptance_criteria":"- Real incident traces can be replayed and compared against synthesized mitigations with expected-loss deltas; promoted mitigations require signed rollout and rollback contracts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CrimsonCrane","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:37:04.007645237Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-22T05:30:32.502727121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T05:30:32.502693739Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["plan","section-10-17","self-contained","test-obligations"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-383z","depends_on_id":"bd-2o8b","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-20T07:43:18.825966610Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
